title: Cisco Quality of Service (QoS)
description: Enable MLS QoS, build class maps and policy maps, apply hierarchical queuing with priority and bandwidth classes, and verify QoS policies on interfaces.

tasks.prompt: Globally enable MLS QoS on switch SW1.
tasks.explanation: MLS QoS must be enabled globally before trust boundaries, classification, and queuing policies take effect on Catalyst switches.

tasks.prompt: Create a class map named 'VOICE' that matches traffic if any listed criteria match.
tasks.explanation: Class maps group traffic for policy treatment. 'match-any' triggers a match when any single match criterion is satisfied.

tasks.prompt: In class map 'VOICE', match packets marked with DSCP EF (Expedited Forwarding).
tasks.explanation: DSCP EF (decimal 46) identifies low-latency voice traffic. Matching on DSCP preserves classification applied by IP phones or upstream devices.

tasks.prompt: Create a policy map named 'WAN-EDGE' to define per-class queuing behavior.
tasks.explanation: Policy maps attach actions (bandwidth, priority, shaping) to traffic classes defined by class maps.

tasks.prompt: In policy map 'WAN-EDGE', enter configuration for traffic class 'VOICE'.
tasks.explanation: The 'class' command under a policy map links a class map to a set of QoS actions applied to matching traffic.

tasks.prompt: Assign strict priority queuing to class 'VOICE' using 30% of link bandwidth.
tasks.explanation: The priority command places traffic in a low-latency strict-priority queue. Voice should use priority; limit the percentage to avoid starving other classes.

tasks.prompt: In policy map 'WAN-EDGE', enter configuration for the default traffic class.
tasks.explanation: class-default catches all traffic not matched by other classes. It always exists implicitly in every policy map.

tasks.prompt: Enable weighted fair queuing for unmatched traffic in class-default.
tasks.explanation: Fair queuing distributes remaining bandwidth among best-effort flows, preventing any single flow from monopolizing the link.

tasks.prompt: Apply policy map 'WAN-EDGE' outbound on interface GigabitEthernet0/1.
tasks.explanation: Service policies are bound to interfaces in the traffic direction they police—output policies shape traffic leaving the WAN uplink.

tasks.prompt: Verify the applied QoS policy and class-level statistics on GigabitEthernet0/1.
tasks.explanation: This command displays the active policy, match criteria, queuing actions, and packet/byte counters per class for troubleshooting QoS.
